mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly boosting S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid deterioration in the labor market signals impending economic contraction, triggering risk-off positioning that depresses S&P 500 returns over the subsequent week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or the predictive t-statistic falls below -1.5 over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between industrial production growth and housing starts indicates structural supply-demand imbalance; when INDPRO falls while HOUST rises, inventory buildup pressures corporate margins, reducing S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The interaction term's coefficient becomes statistically insignificant in a rolling 3-year regression, or forward returns do not differ significantly between divergence and convergence regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer prices force the Federal Reserve to maintain restrictive policy, compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive despite sustained CPI acceleration, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 50% over a 5-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: Sequential quarterly growth in real GDP expands corporate revenue expectations, directly lifting equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Rolling 60-day forward returns show no significant mean difference between positive and negative GDP growth quarters, or the signal's Sharpe ratio falls below 0.1 over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp contraction in nonfarm payrolls triggers immediate repricing of recession risk, causing algorithmic and institutional selling that drives S&P 500 down over the following week.
failure_condition: The 5-day forward return distribution does not shift negatively when the trigger activates, or the hit rate of negative returns falls below 45% over a 2-year test.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS remains elevated while RSAFS contracts, financial conditions tighten simultaneously, squeezing corporate liquidity and equity valuations, yielding negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict returns better than a single-series baseline, or the interaction coefficient loses statistical significance in a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained drop in industrial output signals weakening manufacturing demand, which historically precedes broader economic slowdowns and depresses equity returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive despite consecutive monthly INDPRO declines, or the predictive power vanishes during post-2010 service-led expansions.
